'''Michael Joseph McMeeken''' (born 10 May 1994) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|pr}} forward for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He has previously played for the [[London Broncos]], [[Castleford Tigers]] and the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on dual registration at the [[London Skolars]] and the [[Hemel Stags]] in [[RFL League One|League One]].

McMeeken has played for [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level and featured in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|2017]] and [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 Rugby League World Cups]].

==Background==
McMeeken was born in [[Basingstoke]], and raised in [[Fleet, Hampshire|Fleet]], [[Hampshire]], England.<ref>{{cite web |title=From Basingstoke to Perpignan: Mike McMeeken takes the road less travelled to World Cup |url=https://www.thetimes.com/sport/rugby-union/article/from-basingstoke-to-perpignan-mike-mcmeeken-takes-the-road-less-travelled-to-world-cup-zmlqdb5c2 |website=[[The Times]] |access-date=16 September 2023 |date=18 June 2022 |url-access=subscription}}</ref>

He initially played [[rugby union]] from an early age, but was introduced to [[rugby league]] at ten years old, and began playing for amateur side [[West London Sharks]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Smith|first1=Peter|title=Castleford Tigers: McMeeken intent on earning his stripes|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers/castleford-tigers-mcmeeken-intent-on-earning-his-stripes-1-6976592|website=Yorkshire Evening Post|accessdate=11 December 2017|date=28 November 2014}}</ref><ref name="academy">{{cite web|title=Harlequins' Mike McMeekan selected for England academy|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/9609239.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=11 December 2017|date=6 October 2011}}</ref> 

==Club career==
===London Broncos===
He started his professional career at [[London Broncos]], making his senior debut in 2012. During the 2014 season, he scored his first try for the club in a defeat against [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Red Devils too good for Broncos |url=https://www.eurosport.com/rugby-league/red-devils-too-good-for-broncos_sto4147859/story.shtml |website=Eurosport |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=22 February 2014}}</ref> He also spent time at [[RFL League 1|Championship One]] clubs [[London Skolars]] and [[Hemel Stags]] on dual-registration.<ref>{{cite web |title=Gateshead Thunder 24 London Skolars 16 |url=https://www.skolarsrl.com/2014/05/11/gateshead-thunder-24-london-skolars-16/ |website=London Skolars |access-date=17 September 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Bloomfield's road to recovery |url=https://www.oldham-chronicle.co.uk/news-features/11/roughyeds-news/87445/bloomfield%E2%80%99s-road-to-recovery |website=Oldham Evening Chronicle |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=14 August 2014}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
Following London's relegation from Super League at the end of the 2014 season, McMeeken joined [[Castleford Tigers]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Bower|first1=Aaron|title=Cas sign London Broncos duo Moore and McMeeken|url=http://www.totalrl.com/cas-sign-london-broncos-duo-moore-mcmeeken|website=Total Rugby League|accessdate=11 December 2017|date=11 September 2014}}</ref> He then signed a two-year extension, keeping him at Castleford until 2018.<ref>{{cite web |title=Mike McMeeken: Castleford Tigers forward extends deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/34868930 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=19 November 2015}}</ref>

In 2017, McMeeken signed a further two-year extension until the end of the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Mike McMeeken: Castleford Tigers second row signs two-year contract extension |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40505905 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=5 July 2017}}</ref> 
He played in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=7 October 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=Guardian|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref>
[[File:Michael McMeeken.jpg|thumb|right|McMeeken warming up for Castleford in 2019]]
===Catalans Dragons===
In December 2020, McMeeken signed a two-year contract with [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Mike McMeeken: Castleford's England international forward joins Catalans Dragons |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55280804 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=16 September 2023 |date=11 December 2020}}</ref> On 9 October 2021, he played for Catalans Dragons in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

In April 2022, McMeeken signed a two-year contract extension with Catalans.<ref>{{cite web |title=Mike McMeeken: Catalans Dragons back-rower signs two-year extension |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61187218 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=22 April 2022}}</ref>
On 14 October 2023, McMeeken played in Catalans [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 3 May 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] on a four-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Wakefield Trinity|date=3 May 2024|accessdate=29 Sep 2024|title=McMeeken To Join Trinity|url=https://wakefieldtrinity.com/mcmeeken-to-join-trinity/}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2011, McMeeken was selected in the England academy squad.<ref name="academy" />

In February 2017, he was included in the England elite performance squad announced by coach Wayne Bennett.<ref>{{cite web |title=Mike McMeeken quick to appreciate influence of Castleford Tigers' coach Daryl Powell |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/mike-mcmeeken-quick-to-appreciate-influence-of-castleford-tigers-coach-daryl-powell-1781893 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=27 February 2017}}</ref> In May 2017, McMeeken made his international début for the [[England national rugby league team|England]] senior team in the 30–10 victory over [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in [[Campbelltown Sports Stadium|Campbelltown]], Australia.<ref>{{cite web |title=England v Samoa: Chris McQueen & Mike McMeeken to make international debuts |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/39804623 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=4 May 2017}}</ref> He was also selected in the [[2017_Rugby_League_World_Cup_squads#England|England squad]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|publisher=Mirror|date=9 October 2017|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref> making one appearance during the tournament in England's 36–6 win against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Fixtures |url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/index.php/fixtures/793 |website=European Rugby League |access-date=17 September 2023}}</ref>

McMeeken was recalled to the England setup in 2022, and was selected for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]]. He played in four of England's five games, scoring his first international try against [[Greece national rugby league team|Greece]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League World Cup: England rout Greece with 17 tries as Dom Young helps secure Group A top spot |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/england-vs-greece/56205 |website=Sky Sports |access-date=17 September 2023 |date=29 October 2022}}</ref>

In 2024, McMeeken played from the bench in both tests during the two-match home [[2024 Samoa rugby league tour of England|test series]] against Samoa.<ref>{{cite web |title=England 34-18 Samoa | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP4548093 | website=BBC Sport | access-date=3 November 2024 | date=27 October 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | title=England 34-16 Samoa | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP4548094 | website=BBC Sport | access-date=3 November 2024 | date=2 November 2024}}</ref>

On 13 October 2025, McMeeken was selected in the 24-man England national rugby league team squad for the forthcoming Ashes Test series against Australia national rugby league team.<ref>{{cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |title=Wakefield Trinity duo included in England squad for upcoming massive Ashes series |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-duo-included-in-england-squad-for-upcoming-massive-ashes-series-5357485 |website=Wakefield Express |date=13 October 2025 |access-date=19 October 2025}}</ref>
